_readme: Pre-registered model-change evaluations. Each checkpoint has an explicit pass/fail rule and an evaluate_after date. The weekly health routine (docs/health_routine_prompt.md) evaluates these via tools/analysis/health_report.py and reports status — it NEVER implements changes itself. Add a checkpoint here whenever a model change ships or a candidate tweak is queued; mark resolved ones with status + resolution instead of deleting them.

checkpoints.title: Jul 4 optimization package health
checkpoints.on_pass: Package confirmed — keep all Jul 4 constants and the promotion.
checkpoints.on_fail: If budget CLV < 0 persistently or promoted wr <= demote threshold: demote the dog_better_starter promotion and re-run the constants backtest on the fresh window.
checkpoints.rationale: Jul 19 eval: FAIL on budget CLV (-0.43%) but promoted wr 56% above demote line; demotion NOT executed (the on_fail clause predates better evidence — pattern holding, package at -4% vs old -12%). Keep monitoring; re-evaluate Aug 2 with pattern_card.

checkpoints.title: Candidate: MLB_HOME_ADVANTAGE 0.015 -> 0.0
checkpoints.rationale: Market prices home advantage; adding it double-counts (same mechanism as the refuted injury adjustment). Survived June split-half (+5.0/+4.2pp vs shipped) but is a second-generation tweak mined from June data — this out-of-sample window is its honest test.
checkpoints.resolution: FAILED out-of-sample Jul 19 (see health report): variant worse than live on Jul 5+ window. Discarded per on_fail.

checkpoints.title: Candidate: MLB_RUNLINE_SIGMA 3.5 -> 3.0
checkpoints.rationale: Empirical MLB final-margin SD ~3.1; 3.5 over-flattens run-line probabilities. Survived June split-half (+5.6/+7.2pp) but same second-generation caveat as ha_zero.
checkpoints.resolution: FAILED out-of-sample Jul 19 (see health report): variant worse than live on Jul 5+ window. Discarded per on_fail.

checkpoints.title: Pattern-concentrated card (bet the patterns, skip the rest)
checkpoints.rationale: No constants combo makes a forced full 5-pick MLB slate profitable at 4.5% friction; the profitable pockets are the promoted pattern and large edges. Budget = promoted picks + edges >= 0.08, variable card size. ⚠️ CORRECTED Jul 19: original window (2026-06-13) included the pattern's DISCOVERY data — the Jul 18 'PASS' (58.5%/+2.1%) was in-sample contamination; the July-only out-of-sample slice was 48.6%/-15.8% (n=37, worse than live). Window reset to Jul 5+, min_picks raised to 80, re-evaluate Aug 2. Lesson: a checkpoint's data window must NEVER include the data that generated the hypothesis.
checkpoints.on_pass: Propose to user as budget policy (display/watchlist unchanged): budget accepts only promoted picks and edges >= 0.08; skip thin days.
checkpoints.on_fail: Keep the current 5-pick card with the 0.05 floor.

checkpoints.title: Candidate: promoted pattern picks bypass the 5% budget edge floor
checkpoints.rationale: Design tension surfaced Jul 10 (user-observed live case): SD Padres +1.5 was a promoted dog-with-better-starter pick at 4.8% edge - ranked 3rd on the MLB tab (confidence-first) but vetoed from the budget by BUDGET_MIN_EDGE=0.05; its replacement (Royals +1.5, 6.1% generic spread) lost while the Padres pick led. The promotion's qualification is the PATTERN, not the edge size - if the pattern validates at the package_health checkpoint, sub-floor promoted picks may be exactly the bets the floor should not veto. This variant lets promoted picks with any positive edge enter the budget.
checkpoints.on_pass: Ship: budget routing admits promoted (dog_better_starter) picks with edge > 0 regardless of BUDGET_MIN_EDGE (implement in main.py budget filter; stamp behavior into decision log features).
checkpoints.on_fail: Keep the floor absolute; promoted picks below 5% stay display-only.
checkpoints.resolution: FAILED out-of-sample Jul 19 (see health report): variant worse than live on Jul 5+ window. Discarded per on_fail.

checkpoints.title: FLIP TOTALS idea (Jun 14) — bet the opposite of the model's totals pick
checkpoints.rationale: Original idea: fade the model's totals picks (+5.3% ROI backtested pre-fix). Deferred because the Jun 13 source fix (league_avg_runs 4.5->4.35) could change the bias direction.
checkpoints.resolution: RESOLVED Jul 6 2026: the Jul 2 input-by-input analysis answered the deciding question — the source fix OVERSHOT. The model now has an UNDER bias (~99% of made totals are Unders, projected total avg 1.7 runs below the line, CLV still -2.1%). Flipping is NOT validated in either direction (unders still WIN 52.5% — they lose on price, not direction; and pre-fix flip evidence was for fading OVERS which barely exist now). Decision: totals stay CLV-gated out of the budget; the real fix is the run-expectation level (league_avg_runs / xFIP deflation / _TOTAL_MARKET_ANCHOR) — a candidate for a future registered checkpoint if totals are ever worth un-gating.

checkpoints.title: NBA constants re-audit with full logging infrastructure
checkpoints.rationale: NBA was the historical profit engine (+21.7% ROI in-season) but its constants have NEVER been audited with the decision-log infrastructure (no logged model inputs existed before Jun 13 2026 — NBA season ended Jun 13). When NBA returns (~late Oct), the decision log will capture full features from day one. After ~2-3 weeks of games: run the same reconstruction sweep MLB got Jul 3-4 (tools/analysis/backtest.py needs an NBA variant — reconstruct from NBA features), validate rating-edge weights, rest/B2B, injury handling (NBA injury adjustment is position-only — the MLB value-weighting fix was never ported), and credibility cap. Also verify the hot-streak question: was +21.7% real edge or variance? CLV data will now answer it. EXECUTION NOTE (Jul 15): run this audit with dynamic workflows + adversarial review (user pre-approved — parallel sweep segments, independent agent attacks the methodology for overfit/look-ahead/vig errors before conclusions are presented). Also suggest the user runs /code-review ultra on whatever package ships from it.

checkpoints.title: ERA-trap bonus: should it use ERA-FIP instead of ERA-xFIP?
checkpoints.rationale: FIP-blend hypothesis REFUTED Jul 5 (backing FIP-xFIP>1.0 starters is the BEST segment: 58.1% wr, +6.6% ROI, n=105 — never blend FIP into the quality score). Residual: within own-starter ERA-xFIP>1.0 picks, HR-driven gaps ran worse than sequencing-driven (52.3%/-4.4% vs 58.1%/+5.0%) at n=43 — re-test when both buckets reach ~100.
